h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner gradually resolves confusion about marking values vs headers by position, with no sudden shift.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The word can sound like a header. Do not classify by the word. Classify by the cell position.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor repeats the same positional rule without introducing a new mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot turn exists, so no adaptation to revised framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot turn exists, so no adaptation to revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The word can sound like a header. Do not classify by the word. Classify by the cell position.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor repeats the same positional rule without introducing a new mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The word can sound like a header. Do not classify by the word. Classify by the cell position.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor repeats the same positional rule without introducing a new mechanism.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue placing labels and asking clarifying questions about arrow directions.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Both cards reach the same contact. If this surface is the table, leave one contact label on the cart. Choose the card that stays, then read the cart’s labels again.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's confusion by introducing a physical comparison of the cards, shifting from labeling to discrimination.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn, so no contingent adaptation.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn, so no contingent adaptation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Both cards reach the same contact. If this surface is the table, leave one contact label on the cart. Choose the card that stays, then read the cart’s labels again.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's confusion by introducing a physical comparison of the cards, shifting from labeling to discrimination.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Both cards reach the same contact. If this surface is the table, leave one contact label on the cart. Choose the card that stays, then read the cart’s labels again.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's confusion by introducing a physical comparison of the cards, shifting from labeling to discrimination.
